notes: DROPPED 'talks' (249 titles, 28.1% Iran co-tag = ~2x the 15.7% corpus baseline). Samples confirmed real leak: 'China backs mediation efforts in Iran-US talks', 'Wang urges continued efforts to advance Iran-US talks'. Generic verb mistranslating into iran_theater.

DROPPED 'Beijing' (381). Samples show it is a metonym for the Chinese government across every atomic ('Beijing's nod for H200', 'Beijing's new supply chain rules') plus pure noise (Tiffany flagship). Country synonym => handled by centroid (Pillar 2).
DROPPED 'Trump' (2115 = 35% of the dyad corpus). Vocab-spec rule 4 and hopeless promiscuity.

KEPT 'summit' despite 15.1% Iran co-tag: that is BELOW the 15.7% corpus baseline, and all 14 sampled Iran-co-tagged titles are legitimate US-China summit stories that merely list Iran as an agenda item. Benign co-occurrence, not leak.

'Xi' is a DELIBERATE, MEASURED exception to vocab-spec rule 6 (no 2-char tokens). The EN matcher gives prefix semantics (~* '\mXi'), which costs 14 collateral titles out of 1096 (1.3%: Xiaomi/Xinhua/Xi'an/Pope Leo XIV). Shortest-unique 'Jinping' would match only 174 of 1082 (loses 84%). Inside the USA+CHINA dyad gate, 'Xi' reads as leader-level relationship coverage, not a generic China anchor -- chip/tariff stories say Nvidia and tariff, not Xi. Re-check if the centroid gate is ever widened.

DROPPED 'Wang Yi' (round 2, after reading attributed samples): 18 titles, 9 of them Iran (50% -- 3x the 15.7% corpus baseline). As China's foreign minister he comments on every file ('China opposes military strikes against Iran', 'Wang Yi cites US-Iran progress', 'phone talks with Iranian, French, Omani counterparts'), so he is a promiscuous own-side actor rather than an anchor for THIS phenomenon. The ~7 genuinely bilateral titles mostly carry 'Xi' or 'summit' anyway. 'He Lifeng' kept (3 titles, 0 Iran -- the trade principal, not a general spokesman).
